title: Troubleshooting devices that will not power on
content: Ask the customer to confirm the device has charged for at least 30 minutes with the original cable or power adapter. Have them try a different wall outlet and check for visible cable damage. If the device still shows no lights, sound, vibration, or display activity, collect the order number, serial number, and photos of the charging port before starting the warranty review.

title: Physical damage and warranty review
content: Physical damage includes cracked screens, broken hinges, bent connectors, liquid exposure, rattling parts, or burned smells. Support can collect photos and basic troubleshooting details, but replacement approval requires human review. Do not promise a free replacement until warranty eligibility and damage cause are checked.

title: App crashes, freezes, or will not open
content: For app crashes, ask the customer to update to the latest app version, restart the device, and confirm the operating system version. If the app still crashes, collect the product model, app version, operating system, steps to reproduce, and any screenshots. Known app defects should be logged for engineering with reproduction steps.

title: Sync or settings changes not saving
content: When settings do not save or sync, ask the customer to sign out and back in, confirm they have a stable connection, and check that the app has required permissions. If multiple devices are involved, ask which device shows the correct data. Escalate only when the issue affects multiple accounts or repeats after reinstalling the app.

title: Wi-Fi pairing checklist
content: Most smart devices require a 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network during setup. Ask the customer to keep the product near the router, disable VPN during pairing, and confirm the Wi-Fi password. If the router combines 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz under one name, suggest temporarily separating the bands or using a guest network for setup.

title: Bluetooth connection drops
content: For Bluetooth drops, ask the customer to forget the device from Bluetooth settings, restart both devices, and pair again from the app if required. Check battery level and distance from the phone. If the device disconnects from several phones, collect firmware version and product serial number for escalation.

title: Video calls dropping or home network instability
content: For calls or sessions that disconnect repeatedly, ask whether the issue happens on multiple devices, multiple networks, or only at one location. Have the customer restart the router, move closer to the access point, disable VPN temporarily, and test on another network if available. If the issue happens only on a home network but not elsewhere, collect device type, app version, router model, connection type, and approximate disconnect frequency before escalating.

title: Password reset and login help
content: Customers who cannot sign in should use the password reset link on the login screen and check spam or junk folders for the email. Confirm the email address on the order if they are unsure which account was used. Support should not ask for passwords or verification codes.

title: Locked account verification
content: If an account is locked after too many login attempts, ask the customer to wait 30 minutes and then reset their password. If they no longer have access to the email address, collect order details and proof of purchase for human verification. Account ownership changes must be handled by a human.

title: Lost authenticator or ordinary 2FA recovery
content: If a customer knows their password but cannot complete a two-factor authentication prompt because they replaced their phone, lost an authenticator app, or no longer have access to a trusted device, guide them through the standard account recovery process. Confirm whether they still control the account email address and whether there are any suspicious logins, unknown purchases, or password changes. If there are no compromise indicators, provide the normal recovery steps and explain that support will never ask for passwords or one-time codes. If the customer reports suspicious activity, unauthorized purchases, or a password change they did not make, treat the case as a security concern and escalate for human review.

title: Missing app history or saved settings
content: Ask whether the customer recently changed phones, deleted the app, reset the product, or signed in with a different email address. Some local-only settings cannot be recovered after uninstalling the app or factory resetting the device. If cloud history should exist but is missing, collect account email, approximate date range, and screenshots.

title: Data recovery expectations
content: Support can guide customers through checking the correct account, restoring from device backup, and re-syncing connected devices. Manual data recovery is not guaranteed and requires human review. Do not promise restored data, especially after factory reset, account deletion, or local-only storage loss.

title: Standard refund window
content: Customers may request a refund within 30 days of delivery if the product is unused or returned in resellable condition, unless a regional policy says otherwise. Ask for the order number and reason for return. Refunds are issued to the original payment method after the returned item is received and inspected.

title: Refund exceptions and damaged items
content: Refund exceptions include defective products, duplicate orders, missing accessories, or delivery damage. Collect photos, order number, packaging condition, and a short description of the problem. Policy exceptions, late returns, and goodwill refunds require human approval.

title: Suspected account takeover
content: If a customer reports unfamiliar login activity, password changes, unknown orders, or changed account details, treat the ticket as a security concern. Advise the customer to reset their password and secure their email account. Escalate to a human for account review and do not disclose sensitive account details in the reply.

title: Phishing or suspicious messages
content: Customers may report emails, texts, or websites pretending to represent the company. Ask them not to click links or share payment details. Request the sender address, screenshots, and message headers if available. Confirm official support channels and escalate confirmed phishing reports for security review.

title: Duplicate charge or pending authorization
content: Some banks show a temporary authorization before the final charge posts. Pending authorizations usually drop within 3 to 7 business days. If the customer sees two posted charges for the same order, collect the order number, charge dates, amounts, and payment method last four digits for billing review.

title: Invoice, receipt, and tax details
content: Customers can usually download receipts from the order confirmation email or account order history. For invoice changes, business tax details, or missing receipts, ask for the order number and preferred billing information. Do not edit tax details after fulfillment unless billing policy allows it.

title: Unwanted subscription, add-on, or protection-plan charge
content: If a customer reports being charged for an add-on, protection plan, subscription, or service they do not recognize, ask for the order number, charge dates, amounts, and the payment method last four digits. Clarify whether the charge is a duplicate, a renewal, an optional add-on, or a service attached during checkout. Do not imply fraud unless the customer also reports suspicious login activity, account takeover, or unauthorized purchases by someone else. Standard billing disputes can be reviewed through the billing process, but goodwill refunds, old charges, or policy exceptions may require human approval.

title: Tracking says delivered but package is missing
content: Ask the customer to check nearby doors, mailrooms, building reception, and household members. Carriers sometimes mark packages delivered up to 24 hours before final drop-off. If the package is still missing after 24 hours, collect the order number, shipping address confirmation, and any carrier case number.

title: Delayed shipment or address correction
content: If tracking has not moved for several business days, check the carrier status and estimated delivery date before escalating. Address corrections are only possible before fulfillment or when the carrier supports interception. Once an order has shipped, support can guide the customer through carrier options but cannot guarantee rerouting.

title: Product compatibility questions
content: For compatibility questions, confirm the exact product model, region, device operating system, and any connected accessories. Provide the relevant compatibility limits and avoid guessing about unlisted third-party devices. If the customer has not purchased yet, keep the answer informational and low urgency.

title: Warranty coverage overview
content: Most products include a limited warranty against manufacturing defects for the stated warranty period from purchase. Warranty does not usually cover accidental damage, misuse, unauthorized repair, normal wear, or consumables. Ask for order number, purchase date, and product issue when the customer wants a warranty eligibility check.

title: Returns process questions before requesting a refund
content: When a customer asks whether returns are accepted, how the return process works, or how long a refund usually takes, treat the case as an informational returns question unless they explicitly ask to start a return or receive money back now. Explain the standard return window, item-condition requirements, and that refunds are usually issued to the original payment method after the returned item is received and inspected. Ask for the order number only if the customer wants to begin the return process.

title: Low-signal issue intake
content: If a customer says a product is acting weird, not working properly, or asks for help without describing a concrete symptom, do not assume a specific hardware, software, connectivity, billing, or account issue. Ask for the exact problem, when it started, any error messages, the product model, recent changes, and what troubleshooting steps they already tried. Keep the reply neutral and avoid promising replacements, refunds, repairs, account actions, or technical conclusions until more details are available.
